message:
fix: correctness fixes in progress certificate, oneshot, pool, and happy eyeballs

Four independent bug fixes across core library modules:

- src/cancel/progress_certificate.rs: Fix Azuma–Hoeffding bound computation.
  The lambda parameter was using expected_remaining.max(0.0) when it should
  use (-expected_remaining).max(0.0) — the concentration bound measures how
  far the supermartingale has drifted below zero, not above. Also fix the
  corresponding test: run enough steps (115 instead of 50) so that expected
  potential actually goes negative, making the bound meaningful. Applied to
  both the Azuma and Freedman bound implementations.

- src/channel/oneshot.rs: Fix waker registration logic in RecvFuture::poll().
  The previous match-based approach could fail to re-register a waker when
  the waiter ID matched but the existing waker was None (e.g., after a
  waker was consumed by a send). Restructure into explicit if/else branches
  that handle all combinations of (my_id present/absent) x (waker_id match)
  x (existing waker present/absent) correctly.

- src/database/pool.rs: Fix connection leak during async validation. If the
  future returned by is_valid() was dropped (e.g., due to timeout or
  cancellation) while holding a checked-out connection, the connection slot
  was leaked — total count was never decremented. Introduce a ValidatingGuard
  RAII wrapper that ensures the connection is properly discarded and the slot
  count decremented if the validation future is cancelled mid-flight.

- src/net/happy_eyeballs.rs: Fix potential overflow in stagger delay
  computation. Extract compute_stagger_delay() and duration_to_nanos_saturating()
  helpers that use checked arithmetic (checked_mul, saturating_add,
  try_from with fallback to MAX) instead of raw casts that could wrap on
  very large timeout/delay configurations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
